title: edhoc: No EDHOC error message handling (ERR_CODE)
description:
File: python/src/lichen/crypto/edhoc.py

RFC 9528 Section 6 defines EDHOC error messages with ERR_CODE and SUITES_R. Neither EdhocInitiator nor EdhocResponder:

1. Generate error messages on failure (e.g., ERR_UNKNOWN_PEER when signature verification fails)
2. Parse incoming error messages (would crash trying to decode as regular message)

Current behavior:
- On any error, raises ValueError with a description
- No structured ERR_CODE that peers can parse
- Receiving an error message from peer causes cryptic decode failures

Example: If responder sends error message instead of Message 2:
```python
# message_2 = (ERR_CODE, ?SUITES_R) instead of (G_Y || CIPHERTEXT_2, C_R)
initiator.process_message_2(error_msg, peer_pubkey)  # Crashes
```

Impact: No graceful error handling, protocol non-compliance with RFC 9528 Section 6. Production implementations need error message support for debugging and proper failure handling.
notes: Claimed and analyzing edhoc.py and tests. Adding EdhocError support per RFC 9528 §6.
